verdict.note: Browser e2e via the real board-f entry: two FeedSections with evals leading; default chip=video; rows latest-per-scenario fresh; ZERO media elements at rest; expand→annotate; stale collapsed to count badge (113 stale).

Re-measured after data-unify: opening the view fires ZERO extra /api/board fetches (the section rides the app's one poll via props) and latest-per-scenario is scenarioStates — one computation with the badge/tab. Default chip video, title-only rows, j selects / Enter expands / Enter opens the annotator under the panel's Tab focus.

Master-detail e2e: evals group leads the left list (sticky chips head); default chip video; rows title-only (0 media in list); CONSTRUCTIVE one-data-path proof — /api/board cut before navigation and the rows still render from app-held props; selecting the video row renders the annotator detail (the only <video>).

worktree dashboard (:5207 → live :8787) in headless Chromium: evals group leads the left list, chip head sticky, default kind fell back to image (board carries no FRESH video reading — the video-first half is unexercisable on this data), 21 title-only rows with ZERO video/img elements in the list, kind column renders the new text tag (img) after the emoji→tag change, selecting a row opens the right detail pane (chosen reading is evidence-less, so no media there by data, not by bug). /api/board fetched 2× during the window — initial load + one SSE-triggered event reload from live board churn (other workers active), none attributable to the feed

Real browser over live board (126 current entries, 37 blob-less): note chip claims exactly the 6 fresh / 37 total blob-less rows, all tagged 'note'; image chip claims exactly the 20 fresh image rows, zero blob-less; selected note row's detail renders the verdict note as text — 0 video/img elements, no empty-box hint.
Re-measured after the honest-kind change: evals group leads the left list, default chip = image (no fresh video), 20 title-only rows, zero video/img elements in the list.

#/evals: the 'note' chip claims exactly the blob-less rows — 8 rows, every one tagged 'note' (never img/vid). Selecting a note row renders its verdict note as TEXT in the detail pane — no

Real browser #/evals over the live board (148 stale readings), kind=all. DEFAULT view: 164 rows, stale readings PRESENT (dimmed ✓), newest-first — the newest rows are stalePass (3-5h: schema-gate, scan-eval, branchy-drift, annotate-seek 4h), first-stale-idx=0 while first-fresh-idx=18, so fresh+stale are mixed strictly by time (a newer stale reading sits above an older fresh one). Stale chip toggled ON → 139 rows (the stale count), fresh rows drop out; OFF restores 164. No reading hidden by default — the reported disappearing-screenshot bug is fixed.

Real browser #/evals @HEAD: feed IS the .fv-master LEFT list, chips in a sticky head; default kind chip=video (18 fresh video readings exist). 18 rows = latest-per-(node,scenario), fresh-only (104 stale hidden), newest-first (49m,1h,1h…). Title-only: 0

Real browser #/evals over live board, after the stale-un-hiding change. Default kind chip = VIDEO (the fallback now keys off ALL readings, not fresh-only — a stale video still counts). Rows title-only: 0

Real browser #/evals over live board, after the stale-un-hiding change. 'note' chip → every visible row tagged 'note' (8/8: dangling-orphan-visible, dispatch-fallback-chain, …), never img/vid. Re-measured at HEAD cd2e35e (real browser #/evals, live board, kind=all). 165 rows: stale AND fresh both present and mixed strictly newest-first (mixed=true). Stale chip toggled ON → 104 rows (the current stale count), fresh rows drop out; OFF restores the full mixed list. No reading hidden by default. (After freshening several readings today the newest rows are now fresh at the top with stale interleaved below — the ordering is purely by time, never by freshness.)

REPRODUCED at dfa717c2: with a row selected (selection echoed to the canonical URL), clicking the note chip snaps back to all within 200ms. Cause: EvalsPage passes mustShow whenever the CURRENT selection is hidden (pending ? sel : null), so the deep-link widen fires on the human's own filter click — navigation intent and filter intent are one signal. Bare-load (no selection) sticks, which is why earlier probes missed it.

FIXED: the widen key (deepWant) is one-shot per deep-link arrival — set when the address lands, cleared the moment its entry is visible; mustShow no longer derives from the live selection. Verified: with a video row selected, note chip stays ON at +200/+600/+1500ms and the list narrows; the hidden selection re-anchors to the first visible row with the detail + canonical URL following (#/evals/event-detail/annotate-seek-circle-file); a deep-load of a note-kind address still widens to all and renders its eval.

Real headless Chromium (snap /snap/bin/chromium + playwright-core) on worktree vite :5301 → worktree spex serve :8902 (real board: 231 declared-scenario readings). Head carries ONE select.fv-filter, zero chips/buttons; options read from DOM = exactly [video, image, all] — no note, no transcript. 'image' claimed 87 rows, every one cross-checked against /api/board to hold a real image blob (blob-less under it: 0). 'all' = 231 rows = API's declared latest-per-scenario set incl. 51 blob-less + 82 transcript-only. Blob-less rows carry empty kind tag; selecting one renders text-only detail (0 video/img). Same fv-filter class as the issues store dropdown — one shared FilterSelect.
Same rig (headless Chromium, vite :5301 → serve :8902; board has 13 video readings). Dropdown default value = 'video' (13 rows). Rows title-only: 0

Same rig, dropdown on 'all': DOM row order (231 keys) === API latest-per-declared-scenario sorted strictly newest-first — fresh and stale MIXED (148 stale present; e.g. stale 'retract-undoes-a-botched-filing' at idx 34 sits above older fresh rows). The sticky head carries NO control besides the kind dropdown: 0 .ef-chip, 0 buttons, no 'stale' text anywhere in it — the stale toggle is gone; a stale row's only signal is its muted ✓/✗.

Same rig. With a blob-less row selected, picking 'image' (which hides it): dropdown still 'image' 2s later, list narrowed to 87 rows, selection fell to first visible row — never snapped back to 'all'. Separately, deep-loading #/evals/event-detail/annotate-seek-circle-file (a note eval the default video filter hides) widened the dropdown to 'all' and selected exactly that scenario row.
